page: The Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes a substantial 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high density of 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage needs are met by a bundled 7559 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates exceptional single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip single-threaded compression, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression all rank in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-threaded performance is also competitive, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the strong top 25% tier, while the overall stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores sit in the average middle 50% range. Memory subsystem performance is highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score in the top 25%, though raw memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier. For AI workloads, the system achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 420.88 tokens per second on small 135M LLM models, but drops to average tiers for larger 7B and 70B models.

The primary tradeoff of the F64-16amds_v7 lies in its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and massive local storage capacity contrasted with its moderate multi-core compute scaling. Qualitatively, this server offers high resource density for memory-bound and storage-heavy applications, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling but demand large memory footprints. It is ideally suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching layers, data decompression pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server from the Famdsv7 family, powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a high-density memory configuration of 512.0 GB RAM (32.0 GB per core) and a massive 7559 GB local storage disk. Benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10% tier. While multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with the average middle 50%, the server excels in small LLM text generation and memory-bound operations. This hardware profile delivers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large databases, caching systems, single-threaded data processing, and local storage-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC CPU, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and decompression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 features 16 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage platform delivering exceptional single-core performance for data-intensive and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB of RAM and a massive 7559 GB local storage disk
Top-tier single-core CPU, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression performance
Strong memory performance and rapid text generation for small LLM models
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and storage-heavy workloads
